title: Research using accelerometer data found how walking is broken up matters more than how much of it happens: one large study found sustained walking bouts of 15 minutes or more cut cardiovascular events by roughly two-thirds compared to short bursts, while a separate study of older adults found fragmented, stop-start activity, not total daily movement, was what predicted higher mortality risk
translated_title: 使用加速度计数据的研究发现，步行的分解方式比步行的次数更重要：一项大型研究发现，与短时间的步行相比，持续 15 分钟或更长时间的步行可将心血管事件减少大约三分之二，而另一项针对老年人的研究发现，零碎的、走走停停的活动，而不是每日总的运动量，是预测死亡风险较高的因素。
